ideas.title: Graph-Optimized Source Selection with Dynamic Anisotropic Model Assignment
ideas.description: This research synthesizes graph-based optimization for source selection with automated anisotropic model selection to create a unified framework for Earth observation flux calculations. The core idea is to construct a graph where nodes represent potential source observations, and edges encode both spatiotemporal consistency constraints and the compatibility of observations with specific anisotropic models identified from composite scene analysis. The optimization then simultaneously solves for the optimal set of sources to acquire and the optimal assignment of directional flux models to each, ensuring global consistency in both data coverage and physical representation. This results in a closed-loop system that maximizes the accuracy of derived fluxes by co-optimizing the 'what to observe' with the 'how to interpret it' for complex, multi-component landscapes.

ideas.title: Bias-Aware, Multi-Model Ensemble Data Assimilation for Physically Consistent Gap Filling
ideas.description: This adversarially improved system tackles the fundamental flaw of model error propagation in data assimilation for gap-filling. It utilizes an ensemble of numerical weather prediction models within a hybrid data assimilation framework, dynamically combining their outputs based on real-time performance metrics. A key innovation is an integrated bias detection and correction module that continuously compares model short-term forecasts against trusted observations to quantify local systematic errors. The final gap-filled field is generated from a probabilistically weighted fusion of the ensemble members, rigorously minimizing the influence of biased models and ensuring the filled gaps are both spatially coherent and physically consistent with the surrounding high-confidence observations.

ideas.title: Spectral-UNet: EPIC-Constrained Spectral Harmonization for LEO Imagers
ideas.description: This system addresses the fatal flaw of spectral mismatch by using EPIC's stability as a temporal constraint rather than a direct radiance standard. A deep learning model is trained on collocated, multi-sensor LEO observations, using the temporal consistency of EPIC's convolved signal over invariant targets (e.g., deserts, deep ocean) as a loss function to guide the model. The model learns to generate sensor-agnostic surface reflectance spectra, effectively performing a spectral super-resolution and harmonization. This creates a robust, physically consistent calibration link that corrects for bandpass differences, resulting in truly interoperable multi-sensor datasets.

ideas.title: Adapting Super-Resolution from Medical Imaging to Spectral Downscaling
ideas.description: This idea challenges the assumption that the green band is the optimal guide for downscaling SWIR data by drawing an analogy from medical imaging. In multi-contrast MRI, high-resolution scans from one contrast are used to guide the reconstruction of lower-resolution scans from a different contrast, preserving unique modality-specific information. Adapting this solution, we propose using the high-resolution red-edge band as a structural guide to downscale low-resolution SWIR imagery. The algorithm would transfer spatial detail from the red-edge band while employing spectral constraints to conserve the distinct SWIR reflectance properties, potentially yielding more accurate downscaled SWIR data than methods reliant on the green band.

ideas.title: Super-Resolution of Multispectral Bands via Panchromatic-Guided Dictionary Learning
ideas.description: This idea challenges the assumption that green and SWIR bands cannot be downscaled beyond ~10m with high fidelity using panchromatic data. By drawing an analogy from medical MRI super-resolution, where high-resolution anatomical scans guide the enhancement of lower-resolution functional scans via coupled dictionary learning, we adapt this solution to remote sensing. The very high-resolution panchromatic band acts as a structural guide. A model learns a coupled dictionary from co-registered panchromatic and multispectral (green, SWIR) image patches, capturing their joint sparse representations. For new data, this model uses the observed high-resolution panchromatic information to reconstruct and super-resolve the green and SWIR bands to the panchromatic's spatial resolution, surpassing the limitations of conventional pansharpening techniques.

ideas.title: Apply Generative Adversarial Network-based super-resolution from astronomy to SWIR imaging
ideas.description: This idea adapts a solution from astronomy, where Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) are used for single-image super-resolution to correct for atmospheric distortion in ground-based telescope imagery. The analogous solution involves training a GAN on paired low-resolution (e.g., ground-based) and high-resolution (e.g., space-telescope) images of celestial objects to generate high-resolution details. Adapted to SWIR imaging, a GAN architecture would be trained on curated datasets of paired low-resolution SWIR captures and corresponding high-resolution targets. This approach aims to overcome the physical and cost constraints of SWIR sensor hardware by computationally enhancing image resolution, with potential applications in medical diagnostics, material inspection, and night vision, where fine SWIR detail is critical but sensor resolution is limited.

ideas.title: Apply astronomical image deconvolution techniques to enhance satellite composites
ideas.description: This idea adapts a solution from ground-based astronomy, where image deconvolution techniques are used to recover high-resolution details from blurry telescope images degraded by the atmosphere and instrument. The analogous solution involves modeling the Point Spread Function (PSF) and applying algorithms like Richardson-Lucy deconvolution. Adapted to the research context, we propose using deep learning to learn the effective 'PSF' or degradation function of the 5km satellite composite generation process. A neural network would be trained on pairs of native low-resolution composites and coincident higher-resolution imagery (e.g., from a different sensor) to perform a learned deconvolution, enhancing spatial detail beyond the nominal composite resolution by recovering sub-pixel information.

ideas.title: Adaptive Signal Averaging for Temporal Observation Selection
ideas.description: This idea challenges the assumption that the nearest-time observation is inherently the best by drawing an analogy from signal processing. In that field, reconstructing a true signal from noisy samples uses adaptive filtering (e.g., Kalman filters), which weights past observations based on metrics like signal-to-noise ratio, not just recency. Adapted to this research context, the solution is to replace simple 'nearest-time' selection with an adaptive weighting algorithm. All observations within a scientifically justified temporal window are assigned a composite weight derived from instrumental quality flags and atmospheric stability metrics (e.g., seeing conditions, turbulence indices). The output is a weighted composite observation, thereby prioritizing data quality and stability over strict temporal adjacency to improve the reliability of the derived measurement or state.

ideas.title: Adaptive Multi-Scale Fusion for Global 10-m Water Monitoring
ideas.description: This research adapts a solution from computational neuroscience—specifically, models of the hierarchical receptive fields in the visual cortex—to the problem of generating global 10-m MNDWI time series. The analogous solution uses coarse-scale information to guide fine-scale interpretation, mirroring how peripheral vision provides context for foveal detail. The adapted idea implements an operational pipeline that first generates low-resolution global water masks and time-series trends to establish robust spatiotemporal context. This contextual probability layer then actively guides a convolutional neural network designed to fuse multi-temporal stacks of native 10-m Sentinel-2 imagery, intelligently focusing computational resources to resolve the high-resolution water signal. This hierarchical, context-guided fusion approach aims to achieve the target resolution with greater accuracy, robustness to noise, and operational efficiency than a direct downscaling or simple compositing method would allow.

ideas.title: Adapting Adaptive Optics Calibration for PSF Model Refinement
ideas.description: This idea adapts the calibration methodology from ground-based adaptive optics to the challenge of refining the EPIC instrument's point spread function model. In adaptive optics, the theoretical PSF is corrected using real-time measurements from guide stars to account for atmospheric turbulence. Analogously, we propose using in-flight observations of bright, isolated stars—serving as natural point sources—to measure the actual, instrument-induced blurring. The measured stellar profiles will be compared against predictions from the initial PSF model. An algorithmic framework will be developed to compute differential corrections (e.g., to optical parameters like wavefront error or alignment) that minimize the discrepancy. This empirically updated model will then be deployed to significantly improve the accuracy of convolution operations used in scientific data analysis, accounting for on-orbit optical performance that differs from pre-launch expectations.

ideas.title: Sparse Spectral Unmixing for Sub-Pixel Cloud Phase Dynamics
ideas.description: This research direction proposes a novel method to resolve sub-pixel cloud phase interactions by fusing sparse coding with multi-phase radiative transfer. A dictionary is learned from high-resolution spectral libraries, where atoms represent pure spectral-spatial signatures of ice, liquid, and mixed-phase cloud elements. This dictionary is then used to sparsely unmix the lower-resolution SWIR and other reflective bands, effectively performing a super-resolution that separates the contributions of each phase within a sensor's point spread function. The result is an enhanced-resolution product that explicitly quantifies phase-specific optical thickness and effective radius dynamics at a scale finer than the native sensor pixel, providing new insights into microphysical transition zones.

ideas.title: Joint Hierarchical Bayesian Learning for Downscaling with Latent Uncertainty Quantification
ideas.description: This adversarially improved approach develops a hierarchical Bayesian framework that simultaneously learns the spatial downscaling model and infers the latent uncertainty characteristics of the input data bands. The system models the relationship between coarse and fine-scale variables while parameterizing band-specific error distributions, which are estimated from the data's internal consistency rather than supplied as inputs. It employs heavy-tailed likelihoods and structured priors to maintain robustness when observational errors deviate from standard assumptions. This results in a self-calibrating probabilistic downscaling method that produces reliable high-resolution estimates and meaningful uncertainty quantification even with imperfect input data.

ideas.title: Multi-resolution fusion for native-scale feature consistency
ideas.description: This research investigates a deep learning framework that maintains the integrity of native Sentinel-2 resolutions to maximize information retention. Instead of upscaling, the system processes 10m and 20m bands through parallel convolutional streams, extracting scale-specific features. These feature hierarchies are then fused at multiple stages within the network using attention or concatenation mechanisms. The final model is trained end-to-end to perform land cover classification, leveraging both fine spatial details and richer spectral information without destructive resampling.

ideas.title: Physics-Constrained Adversarial Super-Resolution for Remote Sensing Composites
ideas.description: This redesigned approach employs a deep learning super-resolution model trained within a physics-informed adversarial framework. The generator network enhances 5km composites, while a critic network is trained to evaluate the physical plausibility of the output against known properties of the sensor and land surface thermodynamics. The training objective explicitly minimizes the introduction of spectrally inconsistent artifacts, ensuring the enhanced spatial details are radiometrically faithful. The final system produces higher-resolution data that is suitable for quantitative environmental modeling, not just visual improvement.

ideas.title: Physics-Guided Deep Fusion: Recasting Pan-Sharpening as Spectral-Preserving, Panchromatic-Driven Super-Resolution
ideas.description: This research redefines the pan-sharpening problem within a deep learning super-resolution framework but with critical physical constraints. Instead of treating it as generic upscaling, we design a model that explicitly uses the high-resolution panchromatic image to guide the spatial reconstruction of low-resolution multispectral bands. The architecture and loss functions are engineered to strictly preserve the spectral information from the original multispectral data while integrating the spatial detail from the panchromatic band, ensuring the output is both physically plausible and superior to traditional fusion techniques.

ideas.title: Wavelength-Adaptive Algorithm Extension for EPIC's Non-Visible Channels
ideas.description: This research develops a framework to rigorously extend high-precision astrometric and photometric techniques from visible to EPIC's ultraviolet and other non-visible channels. The core innovation is a modular, wavelength-dependent adaptation of the PSF convolution and compositing method, informed by dedicated in-flight calibration of channel-specific detector and optical performance. The system includes tailored source detection and background modeling for the dominant astrophysical processes in each band, ensuring robust results. This approach enables new, cross-spectral science by providing consistently processed and validated data products across EPIC's full wavelength range.

ideas.title: Use Ensemble Kalman Filtering from Geophysics for Intelligent Gap Filling in Composite Materials
ideas.description: This idea adapts a data assimilation solution from geophysics—specifically, the use of Ensemble Kalman Filtering (EnKF) for reservoir characterization—to the problem of gap filling in composite materials. In reservoir engineering, EnKF assimilates sparse, time-lapse field data into an ensemble of subsurface models to predict properties in unsampled volumes and reduce uncertainty. Adapted to composites research, an ensemble of numerical simulations (e.g., from micromechanical or manufacturing process models) would be integrated. Sparse experimental data from sensors monitoring the composite during fabrication or in-service would be assimilated into this ensemble. The method would continuously update the model states, providing a statistically robust, dynamically constrained estimate to fill spatial gaps (e.g., un-instrumented areas) or temporal gaps (e.g., between sensor readings) in the composite's property or damage state map.

ideas.title: Benchmarking Pan-Sharpening with a Geographically Stratified Test Suite
ideas.description: This research expands the original idea by drawing an analogy from clinical drug trials. Just as pharmaceuticals are tested across diverse patient cohorts to ensure broad efficacy, a pan-sharpening downscaling method should be rigorously evaluated across a stratified suite of geographical regions. The analogous solution involves pre-defining and sampling diverse 'cohorts' of landscapes and water bodies. The adapted idea is to construct a benchmark where the method's performance is quantitatively assessed per stratum—such as arid regions, urban areas, or coastal waters—to identify if the method fails or excels under specific spectral, textural, or seasonal conditions. This stratified analysis moves beyond aggregate metrics to provide a diagnostic 'profile' of the algorithm's robustness and geographical limitations, guiding targeted improvements.

ideas.title: Uncertainty-Aware Joint Inference for Anisotropic Flux Model Selection
ideas.description: This system performs integrated scene analysis and anisotropic model selection through a probabilistic inference framework. Instead of a one-way classification, it evaluates multiple directional model hypotheses against observed local flux patterns and material properties concurrently. A Bayesian model selection criterion automatically chooses the most justified model per spatial region while quantifying the confidence of that choice. This design makes the system robust to scene misidentification, provides built-in uncertainty estimates, and prevents cascading errors by downgrading to safer isotropic models in low-confidence regions.

ideas.title: Confidence-Guided Recurrent Temporal Fusion for Robust Video Downscaling
ideas.description: This system replaces explicit, brittle optical flow with a learned recurrent alignment network that builds a robust temporal representation across multiple frames. It estimates a per-pixel fusion confidence map to dynamically weight the contribution of past high-resolution information, preventing corruption from occlusions or failed motion estimates. The downscaling network is jointly trained with this alignment and confidence module, enabling it to learn when and how to integrate temporal guidance. This results in a video downscaling approach that maintains the benefits of temporal data while being inherently robust to complex real-world video dynamics.

ideas.title: Dynamic PSF Emulation via Telemetry-Conditioned Machine Learning
ideas.description: This research develops a dynamic, state-aware point spread function emulator for the EPIC instrument. Instead of refining a single static model, the system uses a continuous flow of on-orbit calibration star measurements, correlated with a full suite of spacecraft and instrument telemetry, to train a machine learning-based emulator. This emulator predicts the accurate PSF for any target observation based on the concurrent instrumental state (e.g., thermal environment, focus position). The result is a robust, self-updating PSF representation that maintains high convolution accuracy across the mission's evolving conditions without manual re-calibration campaigns.

ideas.title: Predictive Atmospheric-Adaptive Dynamic Scheduling (PAADS)
ideas.description: This system moves beyond passive post-observation weighting to an active, closed-loop scheduling paradigm. It ingests real-time and short-term forecasted atmospheric quality metrics to predict future observing conditions across the sky. An optimization engine then dynamically re-sequences the observational queue, assigning high-priority targets to upcoming high-quality windows predicted to match their needs (e.g., photometric vs. spectroscopic, specific resolution). This minimizes time spent collecting degraded data and maximizes the scientific yield per unit of telescope time by making the acquisition strategy itself quality-aware.

ideas.title: Adapting Multi-Scale Data Fusion from Medical Imaging for Sentinel-2 Band Combinations
ideas.description: This idea proposes extending Sentinel-2 downscaling to diverse band combinations by drawing an analogy from multi-modal fusion in medical imaging. The analogous solution uses specialized network branches and attention-based fusion to combine data of different resolutions and physical meanings. Adapted to remote sensing, a multi-branch deep learning model would be developed, with each branch processing a specific Sentinel-2 band at its native resolution. Features from all branches would be fused using an attention mechanism that learns optimal, context-dependent weighting for generating any target spectral index at a uniform high resolution, moving beyond single-index or fixed-combination downscaling methods.

ideas.title: Sparse Dictionary-Guided Deep Network for Physically-Constrained SWIR Super-Resolution
ideas.description: This research direction proposes a novel architecture that integrates sparse coding and deep learning for single-image super-resolution of low-resolution SWIR composites. A sparse dictionary, learned from a corpus of high-resolution SWIR data, provides a physically grounded prior on the expected structure and material signatures in the band. This prior is embedded into a deep convolutional neural network, either as a regularization term in the loss function or as a structured layer, to constrain the network's solution space. The synergy produces a model that achieves the perceptual quality and detail of deep learning-based super-resolution while ensuring the enhanced outputs are spectrally faithful and adhere to the sparse, elemental representations inherent to the SWIR domain, overcoming the limitations of purely data-driven or purely model-based approaches alone.

ideas.title: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive, Uncertainty-Aware Source Selection
ideas.description: This system addresses the intractability of pre-defining a global optimization graph by decentralizing the selection process. Each source or sensor is managed by an agent that learns, via reinforcement learning, a policy for when to propose an observation based on local spatiotemporal context and uncertainty. A central meta-controller evaluates these proposals using a learned model of global information gain and consistency, dynamically allocating the sensing budget. This results in a robust, adaptive selection mechanism that scales to dynamic environments and complex source interdependencies without relying on a fixed graph model.

ideas.title: Automated Anisotropic Model Selection via Scene-Aware Classifier Ensembles
ideas.description: This research adapts a solution from computational pathology to automate anisotropic model selection in composite materials analysis. In pathology, classifier ensembles identify tissue scene attributes to trigger specialized image analysis algorithms. Similarly, we propose a system where a scene identification module, trained on composite micrograph or simulation data, classifies the material's structural signature (e.g., unidirectional, woven, short-fiber). Based on this classification, a subsequent selection module automatically chooses and configures the optimal directional mathematical model (e.g., for thermal conductivity or permeability flux calculations) from a curated library, eliminating manual expert intervention and improving simulation accuracy and setup efficiency.
